methods: MoDGS (Monocular Dynamic Gaussian Splatting) pipeline
Adoption of single-view depth estimation to guide dynamic scene learning
Novel 3D-aware initialization method for learning deformation field
Proposed robust ordinal depth loss for depth supervision
problems: Novel view synthesis of dynamic scenes from casually captured monocular videos with static or slow-moving cameras
Weak multiview consistency in monocular videos for dynamic scene reconstruction
Inconsistent and insufficient depth supervision across frames in monocular dynamic view synthesis
metrics: Novel view synthesis quality (e.g., PSNR, SSIM, LPIPS, though not explicitly listed in the excerpt)

results.value: MoDGS outperforms state-of-the-art methods by a significant margin on all datasets (exact quantitative values not provided in the excerpt)

novelty_claims: First method to render high-quality novel views of dynamic scenes from casually captured monocular videos with static or slow-moving cameras, overcoming weak multiview constraints.
Proposes a 3D-aware initialization scheme for the deformation field, improving learning of 4D representations from monocular input.
Introduces a novel ordinal depth loss to address scale inconsistency and depth supervision issues across frames, enabling better use of estimated depth maps for dynamic scene geometry learning.

methods: Dynamic Gaussian Marbles: a method for novel view synthesis from monocular videos using isotropic Gaussian 'marbles' instead of anisotropic Gaussians

results.value: Significantly outperforms other dynamic Gaussian baselines; on-par with NeRF-based methods (exact numbers not provided in the excerpt)
Accurately captures 3D motion of scene elements; outperforms other Gaussian baselines (exact numbers not provided)
Maintains efficiency advantages of Gaussian splatting (qualitative claim)

novelty_claims: First method to enable dynamic Gaussian splatting for novel view synthesis from casual monocular videos, not requiring multi-view supervision
Introduction of isotropic Gaussian 'marbles' to reduce degrees of freedom and better constrain optimization in monocular settings
Hierarchical divide-and-conquer learning strategy for globally coherent motion estimation from long monocular video sequences
Integration of freely-available 2D and 3D priors (from SegmentAnything, CoTracker, DepthAnything) into the optimization process for improved geometry and motion estimation
Demonstrates that dynamic Gaussian representations can match NeRF-based methods in monocular settings while retaining efficiency, compositionality, and editability
